Updated: 3/8/2001
Important news in blue



Irwin bought out by Livgroup Investments, Ltd.



Production to start soon on Yu Yu Hakusho and Blue Gender.



Original Dragon Ball to air on Cartoon Network in the Summer and/or Fall of 2001



Season 5 of Dragon Ball Z is currently in production. Air date set for Summer/Fall 2001.



Viz to release future Dragon Ball graphic novels uncensored!



Movie #4, #5, and #6 slated for U.S. release in 2001.



Infogrames will be the publisher of the upcoming DBZ games.


DVD (FUNimation):

Mystical Adventure
(Released)

Androids:
Invasion
(3/13/2001)
Dr. Gero
 (3/13/2001)


VHS (FUNimation):

Perfect Cell:
Temptation
(4/17/2001)
Perfection
(4/17/2001)
Unstoppable
(6/19/2001)

Cell Games:
Ultimatum
(6/19/2001)

Great Saiya Man:
Declaration
(3/20/2001)
Gohan's Secret
(3/20/2001)
Crash Course
(5/15/2001)

World Tournament:
Junior Division
(5/15/2001)

#11
(4/20/2001)
#12
(5/4/2001)

(6/8/2001)

(3/14/2001)

(6/15/2001)

DB Graphic Novels:

(5/18/2001)

Latest Feature: T-Shirt Contest
Thursday - 03/08/2001

Mr.E @ 15:15 pst - Our Petition Worked!
I have been saying this for months and months and months...but it's FINALLY official. DB manga is going to be UNCENSORED! WOO!

AND NOW...A SHOCKING MESSAGE FROM THE EDITOR! As most of you know, back in Dragon Ball Part 3 #3, we announced that due to parental complaints the Dragon Ball manga was going to be "edited" for content. Soon after that, we got hundreds of letters and e-mails begging us not to censor the manga. An online petition to bring back uncensored Dragon Ball was set up by Julian Grybowski, and soon got more than 10, 000 signatures. We listened to your suggestions, and now we are proud to announce:

STARTING THIS MONTH, DRAGONBALL WILL BE UNCENSORED AND HAVE A "13-AND-UP" LABEL ON IT!!!

After much consideration, we've decided that this is the best way to do it. Many of you suggested this (too many to thank you all individually), and you can see the label on this comic and on Dragon Ball Vol. 4 which came out in February. Also, we will soon be reprinting Dragon Ball Vol. 1-3 and re-inserting the material which was censored in the graphic novel version. You can tell which is the uncensored version because it'll have the "13 and Up" label in the lower right corner. The censored graphic novels will not be reprinted and will soon be unavailable.

Because of this label, you might not be able to find Dragon Ball graphic novels at certain large chain stores. (You should still be able to find Dragon Ball Z.) However, we at Viz have decided that this trade-off is worth it-it's better to keep Toriyama's spirit intact. Our thanks go out to all the fans who signed the petition and wrote us intelligent letters saying what you think-it's because of you and all the other Dragon Ball readers that this happened.

Kamehameha!!

*Sniff* Finally, a company that cares. I cannot thank Julian Grybowski enough, who has been in constant contact with Viz trying to orchestrate this goal. He's the mastermind of the whole project, I can only take credit for helping him speed up the process. Julian has also been a very cool guy, and a great person to correspond with.

I would also like to take the time to thank JGonspy, who wrote up this letter for us from DB part 3 #10. But most of all, I need to thank the 10,000+ people who signed the petition to make this happen. It's truely a great day for Dragon Ball fans and anime fans in general.

Mr.E @ 15:10 pst - Irwin Bought Out...
Some speculators saw this one coming...

Irwin Toy, license holder for Dragonball Z action figures, announced Tuesday, March 6 that it had been acquired by Livgroup Investments Ltd., a private holding company. Livgroup bought 100% of Irwin stock, for which they paid $6.25 each, for a total of nearly $55 million, a figure which includes the debt they assumed from Irwin.

This announcement comes nearly a week after rumors that Irwin was in negotiations to sell. There was speculation that one of the major US toy companies, such as Hasbro or Mattel, was the potential buyer; however, Livgroup Investments, owned by investor Richard Ivey and consumer products specialist Jean-René Halde, turned out to be the buyer. Mr. Halde, who has experience in consumer products companies throughout Canada, will step in as new CEO.

"What I like is that they've gone from, 10 years ago, basically being a distributor of products in Canada to today being a manufacturer of exclusive products which seem to be doing well in the U.S. and other countries," Mr. Halde said. From the tone of this and other quotes, it would appear that Irwin will begin pursuing properties such as Dragonball Z, and other properties that promise to do well in the US. This can only be good for Dragonball toys, but we will see for sure in the next few months.

As they said, this can only be good for Dragonball toys. This means Irwin can still produce the figures and not have to pass it to someone else who may be less competent (Unless that someone were McFarlane *purr*). Story credited to the Toy Island.
Wednesday - 03/07/2001

Mr.E @ 23:30 pst - *Updates to Hide Laziness*
What better way to do it than post about an update on someone else's site. VegettoEX has written another DVD review, this time regarding the Android DVD that amazon.com should ship me at any time. Nothing particularily earth-shattering about the disc or the review itself...just more of the same. Read it here.

You might also notice that our banners are back. This means we may actually get *paid* again. And just so you know, I'm still watching the donations total. The 3000 isn't some number I pulled out of my arse, it's a bit less than January's bill alone. Anyhow, I'm hoping to get some cash from these banners to cover future bills. I know they are for casinos and laptops, but hell, they pay. I thank you all again for helping me NOT get into debt...-_-
Tuesday - 03/06/2001

Mr.E @ 20:32 pst - DBZ Cast to Appear at A-kon...
Another appearance for our American VA's, this time in Dallas. You can see the full guest list of A-kon here. Thanks Walter!
Monday - 03/05/2001

Kaio @ 15:56 pst - Why, it's Another Contest! And it's Only Two Months Late!!!
That's right ladies and gents, ye ol' contest section is back and better than ever (it now contains 50% more sodium pentothal). Additionally, a spiffy new T-shirts subsection has been added to our merchandise section. Hooray for clothing! It's been a while since I've done some random linkage.
Sunday - 03/04/2001

Mr.E @ 23:14 pst - Official Site...Toned Down?
Here's a bit of news for the odd category: Apparently FUNimation's official DBZ site is no longer quite so flash-happy. That's right, they've gone back to plain HTML (Except the character animations). This is very strange...perhaps their bandwidth bills were too large? Too many people didn't have flash (Though that shouldn't pose a problem with the "HTML" version of the site)? Peculiar indeed. Skitz spotted this one...I sometimes wonder if he gets sleep. Oh and, to those who wonder why I post such menial news: You're the ones who submit it to me... -_-

Mr.E @ 17:26 pst - Beckett Shocker!
VegettoEX's expose on the fairly shabby Beckett DBZ guide! Fat Elvis found alive in Ft. Worth voicing Majin Buu! SkullMac gives birth to alien baby! These stories and more in today's issue of The Namek Inquirer!

Mr.E @ 16:41 pst - Megacon Spy Report...
They're everywhere! This report comes from Evil Kuno, who had the opportunity to go to Megacon. It's very well-written and informative. I can't thank him enough for it.

First off, Eric Johnson couldn't make it due to illness. According to Sean Schemmel, he was doing some voice overs for Trunks back in Dallas and literaly passed out. From what I heard he had a viral infection and and Sean was little worried. (Kind of ironic that the VA of Trunks suffering from a viral infection instead of the VA for Gokou.)

On Saturday after going through all the booths of awesome merchandise I finally came to the Funimation booth. It was kinda smaller than I expected but it still looked alright. There stood a rep of Funimaton selling merchandise and Sean Schemmel. Before Sean started signing autographs I was able to speak with the rep about my own thoughts and opinions on the dub. I told him how I didn't like the music very much and he was suprised at this. "You don't like the music?" he said. I then told him how it sounded like it was composed on a casio keyboard. And them I told him I grew up on the original soundtrack and he understood by replyng that it was like "reading the book and then watching the movie version". A lot of questinons I asked were answered with "That's up to production" So I sighed and moved on.

Being about the sixth person in line I couldn't help but notice how well Sean was with the fans. He really gets into everything and seems to enjoy every minute of the fan interaction. He would do his Gokou voice and....his Kaio-sama voice at request. But hearing it in person I couldn't help but laugh in enjoyment and horror at the same time.

Finally my turn came. I walked up to him and shook his hand and told him that at first I hated his guts but that I really thought he improved in season 4 and that he's doing a great job as Gokou. He thanked me greatly (I think it meant a little more coming from a 'hardcore' fan, just my opinion) I talked to him about his interview with John on Daizenshu EX for about five minutes and then asked if there was a chance, would he be willing to do a redub of seasons one and two and release it on a bi-lingual DVD just like the ones now. He said he'd love to do it so that he would have all the episodes under his belt and be Gokou through the whole series, but since they're selling so well that it probably won't happen. But that was just his opinion. I also asked him about the Holland dub. He replied "I think it sucked" and he also mentioned that they copied the Funimation script word. Aparently there were lines that Sean and Chris Sabat changed on the fly the day they were recorded. So in reality the only difference in the Holland dub is the voices. The last thing I mentioned was the possibility of having the "secret" out take clips on upcoming DVDs. He said that they were supposed to be top secret but there might be a chance. Some extras that he mentioned for future DVDs are interviews with himself and Gen Fukunaga.

Later I went to the Q&A session where they were going to preview the uncut version of Great Saiyaman: Declaration. Sean had said that he was told it turned out well, but I would be the judge of that. During his Q&A most of his questions were stuff already answered in the Daizenshu interview. He did a few impressions but his most "interesting" one was Christopher Walken doing the next episode preview of DBZ. I couldn't help but laugh along with the rest of the audience. Why I laughed is something I'll contemplate later. But now for the dub itself. First was a bunch of previews for DBZ sagas. THEN came the commercial for the Trunks Saga DUB OFFICAL SOUNDTRACK! "NOW YOU CAN LISTEN TO YOUR FAVORITE DBZ MUSIC OVER AND OVER AGAIN!" excalimed the announcer. But then there was a preview for Blue Gender. WOO HOO! Then came the opening sequence. HOLY The OP sequence is almost left untoucehd! The music is still the same casio crap...but there were only two edits I could make out, which I believe was to cut the OP to a minute. Same goes for the ending sequence as well. As for the eps themselves. Overall not bad. I will now pull a Chris Psaros with an SDA alert

Gohan: Let's boogie! (almost as annoying when Krillin says 'Bro')

Gohan: (Raps as Great Siayman) said during the filming of the Saiyaman movie. Yes, hard to believe that Great Saiyaman has even more cornier dialogue ne?

Gohan: This just in, Gohan, the son of the former champion Goku will not be competing in the tournament becasuse his mommy won't let him.

(it sounded bad to me so I thought it was mentionable)

They messed up the scene where Videl offically discovers that Gohan is Saiyaman. They just had Videl say a bunch of stuff instead of tricking Gohan into admiting it.

Also in the scene where Gohan tells Chi Chi about the Tournament she acts totally against it untill right before the house stops shaking instead of being completely supportive like in the original. (Oh and "zeni" the form of currency in the DBZ world was translated as is)

In the preview for the next episode we hear the announcer talk about Videl needing "happy thoughts" to learn how to fly and how Goten "has plenty of happy thoughts as well"

But aside from that I thought everything else was word for word. Not bad Funimation. I gave my insights to Sean and then I took my leave.

I'd like to mention that Sean Schemmel is a really nice guy. He really takes his job seriously and is doing a better all the time. So don't take your anger out on him since he is not really the one at fault, it's whoever writes the script. Thanks for reading.

Evil Kuno

As you can see, there's a lot of personal opinion in there, but that's OK. Seems that Blue Gender is going to have its music redone as well (ugh), unless the original score is of DBZ dub quality. Now, I'm not a fan of the series, but I'm against all forms of butchery. Anyhow, Evil Kuno is my personal Jesus Christ at the moment (Send hate mail to VegettoEX@aol.com).

Mr.E @ 15:13 pst - BFP Shots...
Three more shots of their latest map have been posted on Bid For Power's main page. Yep.
Saturday - 03/03/2001

Mr.E @ 10:16 pst - Suncoast = The DEVIL...
Alright, you win EX. Suncoast seems to be shipping the two android DVDs, ahead of schedule once again. I'm quite surprised FUNi hasn't taken any action, as I know they don't like it when vendors out-release them. Just search Suncoast.com and you'll find them.

On a related note, Suncoast has yet to ship Mystical Adventure and the Bardock Special DVDs to me. Perhaps they aren't so great after all...
Friday - 03/02/2001

Mr.E @ 18:33 pst - More Polls than a Strip Bar...
I was very surprised by our last poll's results...only a small minority of our visitors are fresh blood. The rest of you guys are old school. Thanks for your support, everyone!

With Cartoon Network airing the original Dragon Ball later this year, there's been a debate over which should have aired first, DB or DBGT. My opinion lies with Cartoon Network's decision, I believe the original series is a much better show. But you guys don't care what *I* think, so take it to the poll!

Mr.E @ 17:50 pst - Brand New Section!
I've taken the best part of our odd e-mails, the cosplayers, and gave them their own section. Now it's MUCH easier to find people making asses of themselves! But don't let me be the judge, let's see what our friendly neighborhood SkullMac had to say:

SkullMac: I want to... hang myself

A rousing endorsement indeed. There's new stuff in there too (like our current image of the moment), so check it out!

Mr.E @ 16:42 pst - CCG Newsletter...
I'll spare you commentary...

10,000 Z Warriors have entered, #10,000 wins big!
The success of the redemption program is overwhelming. We have had to hire some additional Z warriors to assist with the program. BJ Barron wins big as he was the 10,000th person to send in his 4 wrappers. To congratulate and express our thanks for his support of the game, we are sending BJ 15 Trunks Boosters as soon as they become available.

Trunks Common Cards Available Now
Searching for that Saiyan Rare card? We have added in an extra bonus for you! The Trunks Fast Forward Saiyan Boosters should be in stores right now. We have attached a bonus Trunks common card to these boosters to give you a look at the new set before it releases. Pick some up at your local retailer and check out the new Dragonball ® cards.

#1 #1 #1 #1 Dustin Morabito is the leader of the Scoring Zone, #1 #1 #1 #1
Dustin Morabito earns the rank of Super Saiyan today as he leads the Scoring Zone rankings for the DBZ CCG. Dustin plays at Wizard of the Coast game store in Dearborn, Michigan. He has been winning with a Piccolo Anger Deck. Ask your retailer to sign you up for the Scoring Zone today.

Need a place to Play? Store of the Week...
Collect A Card N Comics in Chicago, Illinois stage DBZ CCG tournaments on Saturdays at 1:00 p.m. Contact Lorena Valdez for further details.

[Insert commentary here]

Mr.E @ 15:13 pst - Greg Makes Meaningful Update...Mr.E Dies of Shock...
Although most of this was already revealed on CNX, there is a little bit of new info provided in Greg's Cartoon Network excursion:

Okay, here's what you REALLY want to hear. Toonami info. Six "new" anime series will debut on Toonami before the year is through, and they are: *drumroll please* Outlaw Star (duh), Mobile Suit Gundam (The original series that started it all!!!!), Pilot Candidate, Big O, Gundam 8s AND as I reported just a week ago, although talks are NOT finished yet, DRAGON BALL. Yes that's right. We'll come full circle and start with Son as a child again. Cartoon Network's VP of Programming has told me that they really want to do Dragon Ball as opposed to GT, despite the fact that so many fans want GT, because there's a lot more to work with, (just about a hundred episode more). So there you have it. Cartoon Network has already aquired the last 96 episodes of DBZ and the series will finish up before the year is over. The new episodes of DBZ will be airring this coming summer. Also, expect to see some other anime around the Toonami block as well. Escaflowne will be in there, and the n! etwork is delving into the possibility of Cowboy Bebop. They'd need to edit the series....naturally....and they don't want to show it in a heavily edited format, (quite honorable of them I must say. Ashame others aren't/weren't like that *cough cough* FUNi *cough cough*). On the side of edits, CN really really REALLY wants to make an adult oriented section of programming. But apparently children are watching the station at all times, (wierd), so it's still a no-go as of now.

The new stuff is Cowboy Bebop (!!!) and Cartoon Network wanting to do an adult block. The only thing keeping them from doing so are little kids watching at all hours of the day. Therefore, I am making a call to all those with siblings under the age of 13: Lock your brother/sister in a very dark closet or make sure he/she averts their eyes from your television at all times. THAT will show those Nielsen figures who's the boss! *Ahem* You can read more about Werner's meeting right here.

Oh and, yes, I'm aware that Beckett has stolen some of our images and info. I hold no grudge against them, I just hope they see that mooching off me brings them no personal satisfaction (Alright, maybe a little grudge).

If it was your choice, which series would you want to air on Cartoon Network first?

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragon Ball, nor did we have any part in the production process. There are some items on this website that may be questionable for those under 13 years of age. Dragon Ball was intended for young children in Japan who don't view such material as offensive.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragon Ball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."

-- Napier
